_meta.coverage_note: Top raw signals from GMM sources — unanalysed. Limited qualifying items within 48-hour window (2026-04-09 onwards). Search results lack direct URLs to Tier 1 sources (Federal Reserve, ECB, IMF, Bloomberg, FT, Reuters, PIIE) with explicit publication dates meeting recency threshold.

items.title: US March CPI Release & April University of Michigan Sentiment
items.summary: US March CPI and preliminary April University of Michigan Sentiment Index scheduled for release on 2026-04-10. Key macro calendar event driving volatility expectations and Treasury yield positioning ahead of data.
items.domain_relevance: Central bank policy signal; inflation data; consumer sentiment

items.title: US High Yield Corporate Bond Spreads Tighten to 270bps
items.summary: Bloomberg high yield corporate bond spreads to US Treasuries tightened 10bps from Wednesday to Thursday close, reaching 270bps—down from 335bps on March 30. Signals risk-on sentiment and credit market normalization.
items.domain_relevance: Credit market stress; corporate debt; risk appetite

items.title: China PBOC Gold Reserves Increase 5 Metric Tons in March
items.summary: People's Bank of China increased gold reserves by approximately 5 metric tons in March 2026, marking largest monthly purchase in over a year and extending 17-month consecutive buying streak. Signals central bank hedging amid geopolitical stress.
items.domain_relevance: Central bank reserves; currency hedging; emerging market policy

items.title: Gold Prices Decline 12% in March—Steepest Drop Since 2008
items.summary: Gold prices fell approximately 12% during March 2026, the steepest monthly decline since 2008, driven by Iran conflict strengthening US dollar and shifting expectations toward tighter Federal Reserve policy.
items.domain_relevance: Commodity price shock; USD strength; geopolitical risk

items.title: E-mini S&P 500 Futures Rally Above 6,800; Nasdaq 100 Above 25,000
items.summary: E-mini S&P 500 futures climbed more than 50 points above 6,800 level; Nasdaq 100 futures rebounded strongly, gaining over 200 points above 25,000 after prior risk-off pressure. Signals equity market stabilization despite geopolitical uncertainty.
items.domain_relevance: Equity market stress; risk sentiment; index levels
